assertions.title: No visual regressions on existing surfaces

steps.action: Open / in a headed non-default browser session and inspect the graph-first workspace hero, navigation, and node atlas.
steps.expected: The shared production-backed app loads with the graph workspace, BETTER logotype, and no broken imports or runtime failures.
steps.observed: The app loaded successfully with the BETTER logotype, investor-path control, overview state, and visible node cards over the site-wide atmospheric background; no import/runtime failure banner or broken shell was visible.

steps.action: Traverse the existing major surfaces: What is BETTER, Proof & Trust, Live Now, Roadmap, Tokenomics, Architecture, Valuation, Evidence & Sources, and Risks & Caveats.
steps.expected: Each existing surface renders correctly without layout corruption, broken navigation, or styling regressions.
steps.observed: All nine existing surfaces opened and rendered readable card/panel layouts with consistent typography, borders, and spacing; roadmap branch families and maturity legend rendered correctly, and no obvious layout shift or broken navigation state was observed.

steps.action: Traverse the four new content-depth surfaces: Macro Thesis, HFT Edge, LLM Product, and TRUTH-PERP & Flywheel.
steps.expected: Each new surface appears polished and integrated into the atlas with concrete content and stable layout.
steps.observed: All four new surfaces rendered as styled inspector/detail panels with visible maturity state, related-node chips, and dense content cards. Macro Thesis showed the war/hedging and prediction-market metrics, HFT Edge showed Rust/co-location/Z-Score content, LLM Product showed BRAID/$29 API framing, and TRUTH-PERP & Flywheel showed HIP-3/vBETTER/flywheel content without visible corruption.

steps.action: Check runtime health and network response after traversal.
steps.expected: No console/runtime errors; app responses succeed.
steps.observed: `agent-browser errors` and `agent-browser console` returned no entries. `curl -I http://127.0.0.1:3100/` returned `HTTP/1.1 200 OK`, and in-browser performance entries showed the page loading `/better-logotype.svg`, Next CSS/JS chunks, and font assets from the assigned app origin.

evidence.network: HEAD / -> 200 OK via curl; in-browser resource entries included /better-logotype.svg, Next static CSS/JS chunks, and font assets from http://127.0.0.1:3100

frictions.description: Top-level atlas node buttons are contextual to the current view, so after drilling into one surface I re-opened the root URL between captures to reliably access other top-level surfaces.
frictions.resolution: Reloaded `http://127.0.0.1:3100/` before each top-level surface capture.

summary: VAL-POLISH-001 passed. I visually validated the graph workspace plus all nine existing major surfaces and the four new content-depth surfaces in the shared browser app on port 3100; no console/runtime errors, broken imports, navigation failures, or obvious layout/styling regressions were observed.
